sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! Now there are 0 green cells! All green cells are gone. But there's a new purple node with a white center at the bottom left. Let me check the segmentation.
It seems like moving (16,44) to (16,32) caused all green cells to disappear. This might be the goal - to eliminate all green cells by merging them.
But wait, there's a purple node now. Let me check what it is.
Also, I notice the bottom grid now has a gray cell instead of a green one at (52,44).
Let me check the full segmentation to understand what happened.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check all nodes
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("All nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    cr = sum(p[0] for p in b)//len(b)
    cc = sum(p[1] for p in b)//len(b)
    print(f"  {n['color']}: center=({cr},{cc}), pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the segmentation, I see:
- g: center=(16,32), pixels=12 - this is a gray cell at (16,32)
- g: center=(52,44), pixels=12 - this is a gray cell at (52,44)
- p: center=(57,6), pixels=52 - a purple node
- B: center=(57,6), pixels=40 - a black node (probably the border of the purple one)
- W: center=(55,6), pixels=4 - a white node (center of the purple one)
So the green cells at (16,38) and (16,44) were merged into (16,32), and the green cell at (52,44) became gray. There's also a new purple/white/black object at (57,6).
Wait, let me reconsider. The green cells are now gray (g color). This might mean the level is not complete yet. I need to continue moving cells.
Actually, looking more carefully, there are two g nodes: (16,32) and (52,44). These are the positions where green cells were merged/converted.
Let me try selecting (16,32) to see if there's a g overlay.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Select (16,32) which is now gray
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 32}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('G', 'g', 'N'):
        b = n['boundary']
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in b)//len(b)
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in b)//len(b)
        print(f"{n['color']}: center=({cr},{cc})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
